DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRISTOPHER J. BANKS, Esq. of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP (Tel: 415-442-1000) for Plaintiff
Convexity Limited;
Atty. RICHARD DE BODO, Esq. of Theodora Oringher PC (Tel: 310-557-2009) for Defendants
Wallarm, Inc. and Ivan Novikov.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS IVAN NOVIKOV AND WALLARM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Wallarm, Inc. and Ivan Novikov's demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are overruled. A complaint "is adequate so long as it apprises the defendant of the factual basis for the plaintiff's claim." (McKell v. Washington Mutual, Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1470.) The FAC did so.

The FAC adequately pled breach of fiduciary duty. (Id. at 10:1-11:6, 16:7-18:6.) Defendants seem to argue that agency can never be created by contract, but the cases they cite do not so hold. (Indeed, no case defendants cite here involved a demurrer.) Defendants also argue that plaintiff has not "established the control necessary," but establishing facts is not a complaint's function, pleading allegations is. Further, an independent contractor may also be an agent. (City of Los Angeles v. Meyer Bros. Parking System, Inc. (1975) 54 Cal.App.3d 135, 138.)

The FAC also adequately pled fraud. (Id. at 12:25-16:6.) Two species of fraud are pled with sufficient particularity. (Id.; see also 5:4-6:14 (details of Novikov's activities and communications).) A "party may plead in the alternative and may make inconsistent allegations." (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.) Thus, fraud and contract claims may be pled in the alternative, and the former are not barred by the "economic loss rule."

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed and submit order which repeats the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
